strict_win_deltas.delta: P2's validateWave() parses and returns fully-typed internal objects (with index fields) that fulfillWave(wave) consumes directly, whereas P1's validateInput() only checks the raw JSON and fulfillWave() then separately re-maps input.warehouses/input.orders into its own normalized shapes, duplicating the data-shape knowledge across two functions.

P1 terminates via process.exit(2) inside writeFulfillWaveError, which can truncate an unflushed process.stderr.write on some platforms, while P2 sets process.exitCode = 2 and lets Node drain stdio naturally before exiting.

P1's warehouseComparator, lotComparator, order sort and remaining sort all use String.prototype.localeCompare (locale/ICU-dependent collation), which can diverge from a plain byte-ordinal 'ascending by id' as literally specified, while P2 defines and consistently uses a deterministic compareStrings(<, >) for every tie-break.

P1 factors allocation into small single-purpose helpers (matchingLots, allocateFromWarehouse, allocateLine, rollback) versus P2's ~70-line monolithic fulfillWave() that inlines candidate filtering, lot consumption, and remaining-stock computation in one block, and P1 also adds a third dedicated single_warehouse-rejection test documenting that edge case for future maintainers.
